summary: ICP/Motoko APA Pool Scorer app. Live canister is v163. Debug panel being added as a small footer link (toggleable, no interference with existing nav/gear icon functionality). Frontend-only deploys succeed; backend deploys still failing — support contacted. Next step: verify owner/admin state via debug panel on live canister.
architectural_notes: Backend uses Motoko with HashMap for role assignments (principal → role variants like #user, #admin)
adminAssigned flag controls whether initialize() can reassign admin role
Debug queries must be read-only (query calls), no side effects, callable via Candid UI
Surgical fix to access-control.mo: case (?_) {} replaced with token-checked admin promotion — only promotes if adminAssigned is false and token matches
No-op deploy test: add comment-only change to backend/access-control.mo to isolate whether deployment failures are pipeline/network issues or code-edit-induced compile errors
Frontend-only deploys succeed while backend deploys fail — confirms issue is backend upgrade/install pipeline, not frontend or network connectivity
Debug panel toggle moved from second header nav button to a small footer link — gear icon retains existing Edit Profile modal behavior
known_issues: Repeated deployment failures (v148+) caused by full codebase regeneration from memory introducing Motoko syntax/type errors
Deployment failures occurring post-fix attempt; no-op test in progress to isolate root cause (pipeline vs. code edit)
No-op deploy (comment-only change) also failed, confirming deployment failures are infrastructure/pipeline/network/state level, not code-related
Owner/admin state on live canister is unconfirmed — 'Claim Ownership' may not have set correctly during prior troubleshooting
Second header nav button (gear icon) already owned by Edit Profile modal — debug panel wired there was shadowed/overridden

existing_features.title: Add a debug panel component that calls getOwner(), getCallerPrincipal(), isCallerAdmin(), isCallerApproved(), and getCallerRole() on the live canister and displays the raw returned values. Each call must be wrapped in individual try/catch blocks so a trap on any single call (e.g. getCallerRole) does not prevent the others from rendering. Show each result as a labeled key-value pair. The panel should be toggleable (show/hide).

Wire the debug panel toggle to the second button in the header nav bar. The element at XPath /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/header[1]/div[1]/div[2]/button[2] (the second button in AppLayout's header nav div) should open/close the debug panel when clicked. Only this button's onClick behavior is changed — no other header elements, layout, styles, or buttons are modified.

In the footer of AppLayout (the area containing '© 2026 APA 9-Ball Scorekeeper. Built with ❤️ using caffeine.ai'), add a small 'Debug' text link next to or below the copyright line. Clicking this link toggles the existing DebugPanel component open and closed. The DebugPanel component already exists at frontend/src/components/debug/DebugPanel.tsx — import and reuse it as-is. The gear icon button must retain its existing Edit Profile behavior completely untouched. No changes to the header, nav buttons, any other layout elements, or any other functionality.

new_feature_requests.summary: In backend/access-control.mo, add a single comment line '// noop deploy test (comment-only)' immediately after the import statements at the top of the file. Make absolutely no other changes to the file — no logic, no formatting, no whitespace modifications outside of inserting this one comment line.

In backend/access-control.mo, add a single comment line '// noop deploy test retry (20min)' immediately after the import statements at the top of the file. Make absolutely no other changes to the file — no logic, no formatting, no whitespace modifications outside of inserting this one comment line.

Add a small 'Debug' link in the footer (next to/below copyright line) that toggles the debug panel open/close. The gear icon must keep its existing Edit Profile behavior untouched. Zero other layout, nav, or functionality changes. Easy to remove later.
